## [00:00 - 03:02]

pretty simple and straight to the point. So basically we're going to be going over what a setter is versus a setter closer versus a essentially a full inbound closer. So there's pretty much three different roles. These are the three different roles that our clients are hiring for really simply the first one is obviously the setter. So the setter role pretty simple, pretty straightforward. The company they offer that this person will be working on as a setter. They have pretty much warm leads and inbound leads coming into their ecosystem. And most of the time these people are coming through basically through paid advertisements that the company is obviously putting out there whether it's on Facebook, YouTube, Google, Instagram, etc. And all these people are coming into either an email list into either like a database or a CRM. So they would obviously be doing some outbound, some warm calls, different things like that, text messaging, things all like that. And then really from there this role is strictly essentially like their whole goal is to strictly reach out to people that are in this clients or this company or this offers ecosystem. Because these people came into this offer because or into their ecosystem because they either need help with something or they're interested in whatever it is they have to offer. And so really the whole goal is for them to essentially reach out to those people and qualify them on a quick 10, 15 minute call, you know, see where they're at, what they're looking to do, where they're looking to go, you know, and get their goals. And then from there, essentially see if what that offer is that the company is selling would be a good fit for that person, as well as obviously qualifying them financially and all of that. From there, the set obviously gets paid once the set actually closes. And there are also some offers where they'll also have a base for the setter. That's pretty much that there for a setter slash closer hybrid. So as I said, this is obviously a hybrid role. This setter slash closer, this person will already be getting anywhere from like one to four inbound calls a day with their application booked onto their calendar.

## [03:02 - 05:21]

And then from the other time that they have during the day, they would essentially be setting their own deals, just like a setter would, it's going to be the exact same process up here as a setter would, except the only difference is that they're booking the call for themselves to take either later on that day or the next day, instead of for a different closer. These people are usually paid strictly on commission, based on the cash they collect. And so on some offers, they also be actually compensated extra for setting their own deals, self generating the deal. On average, you know, the commission really breaks down to 10% of cash collected. So if you obviously collect $100,000 in cash in one month, you get 10%, which is about 10 K. Plus you might also get a little bit of a kickback for setting your own deal. Plus obviously bonuses and different things like that. And that applies to all of these. And the income potential really there is I would say on like the low, low end, six K per month, probably averages anywhere in between that eight to 10 K. As I said, low end six, but this all of this role could, depending on the right opportunity and the right offer, could be way more than 10 K, could be 12, 13, 15. And so that's pretty much that there. And then from there is the strict closer. So essentially this person, they have their call, so they have the calendar open, you know, for anywhere from four to 10 calls per day, they're strictly having calls booked onto their calendar by either the companies or the offer's marketing, or by a setter booking calls onto their calendar. As I said, they're getting anywhere from four to 10 calls a day, depending on the offer. And really the closest goal is just to hop on zoom with the prospect and close them. That's pretty much it. You know, figure out where they're at, where they're looking to go, present them the offer, if it makes sense, obviously the enroll. And the income potential here is really anywhere from like eight to 10 K per month, I would say on average, but obviously that can be a lot more, could be 12, 15, and sometimes even up to 25, 30 K per month, depending on the offer. Exact same here as the setter closer, you know, they're paid on cash collected. So as I said, cash collected 10%. And then also whatever bonuses, you know, they get for hitting certain revenue numbers or cash collected numbers. And that's pretty much it there.
